description: Concept buckets for the concept-first vocabulary pipeline. A concept may belong to one or more buckets, and one observed form may map to multiple concepts or senses.

buckets.description: Closed-class English entities and other linguistic substrate concepts used to analyze phrase structure and meaning.
Organizations, hosted services, platforms, ecosystems, and delivery surfaces.
Named model families, model lines, or related AI model groupings.
Software tools, components, packages, libraries, CLIs, plugins, and reusable building blocks.
Operational processes, pipelines, developer workflows, orchestration patterns, and repeated procedures.
Things a system can do or support, including functional and non-functional abilities.
Tests, benchmarks, evaluation methods, scoring schemes, and measurement-oriented concepts.
Execution environments, deployment substrates, OS/runtime concepts, and environment-level infrastructure.
Rules, policies, guardrails, governance structures, and decision constraints.
Named artifact types such as plans, specs, templates, manifests, datasets, and records.

ambiguity_policy.note: Observed forms and concept meanings are not assumed to be one-to-one. Bucket assignment may be partial or provisional until more evidence is available.
